head.title: Freehold purchase costs | Moonstone Advocates
head.description: Understand your freehold purchase costs with clear, practical guidance. Find out what fees to expect and get expert support from our conveyancing team.
head.ogDescription: Understand your freehold purchase costs with clear, practical guidance. Find out what fees to expect and get expert support from our conveyancing team.

blocks.html:
Pricing overview

Freehold purchase

* For properties with a value of £1million and above, fees will be provided upon request.

Freehold purchase costs

‍
Our legal fees
‍
Bank transfer fee
‍
AML fee
‍
Anticipated expenses**
‍
Lawyer check search
‍
Land Registry search costs (OS1)
‍
Bankruptcy search costs (per name searched)
‍
In addition to the above the following supplemental fees may apply:
  • Buy to let fee £100 (plus VAT)
  • Mortgage fee £150 (plus VAT)
  • Limited Company/LLP fee £175 (plus VAT)
  • New build fee £750 (plus VAT)
** Not listed in these expenses or included in our legal fees is:
  • The HM Land Registry Registration fee, which is based on the purchase price of the property
  • Conveyancing search costs, the cost of which varies depending on the search and provider

‍

‍

Amount(excluding any VAT)

‍
Range from £600 and £1850* (plus VAT)
‍
£45 (plus VAT)
‍
£45 (plus VAT)
‍

‍

‍
£7.50 (plus VAT)
‍

Possible factors affecting fees

Sometimes, extra work comes up during your sale that we could not have predicted at the start. If that happens, it can affect our legal fees or any third party costs you need to pay.

‍

If costs do change, we’ll let you know straight away so you’re always clear on where you stand.

‍

Examples of what might increase costs include:

‍

  • You are selling a share in a property jointly owned by a housing association (shared ownership)
  • The property hasn’t been registered at Land Registry
  • You are repaying a mortgage that’s with a non- high street lender
  • There are problems with the title deeds
  • You are selling the property subject to a tenancy
  • The term of the lease has to be extended as part of the transaction
  • Your lease requires you to obtain a licence from your landlord before you sell the property
  • We have to pay a referral fee to the professional who referred you to us

‍

‍

Need an exact quote?

Contact our Residential Property team to receive a tailored estimate for your specific circumstances.

Call us today

Send an enquiry
